commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

commit.message:
Fix Qwen-7B 'NoneType' size error from pre-created DynamicCache

generate() in transformers 5.x creates DynamicCache(config=...) which
pre-allocates layers with keys=None. Qwen-7B's remote code treats this
non-None cache as populated and crashes on None.size(). Opt Qwen-7B out
of automatic DynamicCache creation so it receives past_key_values=None
on the first forward pass. Also harden __getitem__ patch to return None
for uninitialised layers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Adiciona endpoint específico para transcrição literal de índices

Problema resolvido:
- O mesmo endpoint estava sendo usado para capa (análise) e índice (transcrição)
- Para índice, usuário quer texto LITERAL e não análise do livro
- Claude estava identificando "isso é do livro X" ao invés de transcrever

Nova função processIndexOCR:
- Endpoint POST /api/ocr/transcribe-index
- Prompt específico para TRANSCRIÇÃO LITERAL palavra por palavra
- Instruções claras: copiar texto exato, manter formatação original
- Copiar números de página, pontos, travessões como aparecem
- max_tokens 4096 para índices longos
- Retorna apenas texto transcrito (data.text)

Diferença dos endpoints:
- /ocr/analyze-cover: ANALISA capa → retorna {titulo, autor, editora, ano}
- /ocr/transcribe-index: TRANSCREVE índice → retorna {text}

Exemplo do prompt:
"Transcreva EXATAMENTE todo o texto visível
Mantenha formatação original (quebras de linha, pontos, etc)
NÃO analise, NÃO interprete - apenas COPIE o texto"

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Inject vendor data server-side into profile page — eliminates client fetch

GET /vendors/:id now reads vendor from DB and injects window.__PITCH_VENDOR__
into the HTML before sending, same pattern as serveDashboard(). The page
arrives from the server already containing its data — no extra round-trip,
no waiting hidden. Instant reveal on script execution.

sessionStorage + fetch remain as fallbacks for edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove season check — GitHub delays crons 5+ hours

The ±90 min season check was rejecting legitimate but delayed
crons (midnight cron observed arriving at 5:12 AM = 312 mins
late). Firebase dedup is the only guard needed: first cron to
fire sends the push, second finds data already captured and
exits silently. Reliability over precision.

commit.message:
fix(netpol): allow intra-namespace traffic for pravara-mes postgres/redis/emqx/centrifugo

pravara-mes deploys its own postgres-pravara, redis-pravara, emqx, and centrifugo
pods in-namespace, but all egress rules targeted the shared data namespace —
blocked by default-deny. This caused pravara-api CrashLoopBackOff (DB connection
refused) and mes-api.madfam.io 502.

Changes:
- Add 4 new ingress policies: postgres-pravara, redis-pravara, emqx, centrifugo
- Fix all 7 egress policies to target local podSelectors (not data namespace)
- Add centrifugo:8000 egress to pravara-api, emqx:1883 to telemetry/mqtt workers
- Update header comment to document in-namespace infrastructure architecture
- Add NetworkPolicy intra-namespace failure mode to OUTAGE_REMEDIATION.md
- Fix cluster-ops-deploy.sh to delete+recreate policies (annotation merge gotcha)

Cluster-side: requires kubectl delete --all + apply in pravara-mes namespace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: v1.1.41 — POST /workflows/agentmesh 4-role dev pipeline

Adds POST /workflows/agentmesh implementing the AgentMesh Planner→Coder→
Debugger→Reviewer 4-role sequential development pipeline (arXiv:2507.19902).

- `AgentmeshWorkflowSubmit` Pydantic schema (feature_request, language,
  scratchpad_prefix, agent_timeout, reply_to) in web/schemas.py
- `submit_agentmesh_workflow` endpoint in web/routers/workflows.py with
  4-step sequential DAG: planner→coder→debugger→reviewer via depends_on
- required_tags routing: agentmesh_planner/coder/debugger/reviewer
- Blackboard pattern: each phase writes to scratchpad (_plan/_code/_debugged/_review)
  and reads all prior outputs (reviewer reads all 3 previous keys)
- 44 new tests in tests/test_workflow_agentmesh.py (2798→2842 total)
- OpenAPI snapshot updated
- Demo (v1.1.41-agentmesh): 36/36 PASS; binary search pipeline completed;
  reviewer gave ★★★★½ (4.5/5); all 4 scratchpad keys populated

commit.message:
fix: stabilize excluded precompiles tests by pausing block builder (#2530)

* fix: use await for txpool assertions in ExcludedPrecompiles tests

Both ExcludedPrecompilesLimitlessTest and ExcludedPrecompilesTest
asserted getTxPoolContent().hasSize(2) immediately after sending
transactions via RPC. Under CI load, the second tx may not yet be
visible in the pool snapshot, causing intermittent failures.

Wraps the assertions in await().untilAsserted to poll until both
transactions appear, matching the convention used throughout the
acceptance test suite.

Fixes #2528

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): resolve sequencer acceptance txpool assertion

* test: remove unused tx hash mapping in excluded precompile tests

* test: preserve txpool size assertion semantics in flaky wait

* fix: pause block building during txpool assertions in excluded precompile tests

Background block builder traces and evicts excluded-precompile txs from
the txpool before the assertion can observe them. Pause block building
before sending invalid txs, assert pool state, then resume - matching
the pattern used by TransactionSelectorDenyListReloadTest.

* fix: remove flaky txpool size assertion from excluded precompile tests

The txpool size assertion races with the Engine API block builder. Even
with buildBlocksInBackground=false, an in-progress build can trace and
evict txs before the assertion runs. The assertion is redundant since
hasError()==false already verifies pool acceptance, and the log assertion
at the end verifies trace-level rejection.

* fix: wait for block cycle completion before pausing block builder

Wait for the current block cycle to finish (block number increment)
before setting buildBlocksInBackground=false. This prevents the race
where an in-progress Engine API block build traces and evicts excluded
precompile txs from the pool before the txpool size assertion runs.

Restores the original txpool size assertion from main.

* ci: trigger acceptance test run #2

Made-with: Cursor

* ci: trigger acceptance test run #3

Made-with: Cursor

* ci: trigger acceptance test run #4

Made-with: Cursor

* ci: trigger acceptance test run #5

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: resolve flaky ExcludedPrecompiles tests with manual block building

Replace race-prone flag + sleep approach with stopBackgroundBlockBuilding()
that shuts down the consensus scheduler cleanly. Tests now use manual
buildNewBlockAndWait() instead of resuming background building, eliminating
the race where scheduleAtFixedRate could start a new block build before
the test set the pause flag.

commit.message:
Fix return_documents=false: strip documents server-side

vLLM ignores return_documents in the request payload and always returns
document text. Handle it in _proxy_rerank_request by stripping document
fields from results when the client sets return_documents=false.

commit.message:
fix: rename private_dot_1Password to private_1Password

private_dot_1Password was mapping to ~/.config/.1Password (with dot)
but the real path is ~/.config/1Password (no dot). The private_ prefix
sets permissions to 700; dot_ adds a dot to the name — we only want
the former here.

fix: blank screen after AI interview and PDF download failure

Bug 1 — blank screen after AI interview:
- resumeParser: add validateParsedData(); applyInterviewAnswers now deep-merges
  AI result with existing data and falls back to original on bad JSON
- ResumeContext: importResumeData preserves existing arrays when parsed arrays
  are empty, preventing wipe-out on partial AI responses
- AIInterviewModal: call importResumeData + useToast directly instead of
  relying on onUpdate prop; error path shows toast and keeps existing data

Bug 2 — "Unable to find element in cloned iframe" PDF failure:
- pdfExport: add downloadResumePDF(elementId, fileName) helper
- PreviewPage: switch Print to window.print(), Download to downloadResumePDF;
  wrap lazy Component in Suspense in hidden print target; add element id
- ResumePreview: use downloadResumePDF with stable element id on hidden div

Initial Laravel 12 project setup for TempMail

Scaffold a fresh Laravel 12.53 project with MySQL configuration,
default migrations, and standard project structure. This will serve
as the foundation for the temporary email service.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add revenue and orders analytics API endpoints

New endpoints:
- /api/revenue/daily - Daily revenue aggregation
- /api/revenue/monthly - Monthly revenue aggregation
- /api/revenue/growth - Month-over-month growth rate
- /api/orders/by-platform - Orders count by platform
- /api/orders/by-category - Orders count by category
- /api/orders/by-dayofweek - Orders grouped by day of week
- /api/aov-distribution - Average order value distribution

All endpoints support filtering via platform, category, start_date, end_date
and use the existing caching layer for performance.

integration tests for all API endpoints using httptest with JWT auth flow.
Frontend: vitest setup with tests for StarRating, FilterChips, Toast
components, i18n utility, and useToast composable. Updated CLAUDE.md with
pre-commit testing rules.
